full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Basic clarification on what removing b actually means.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: ok so in the svm paper when they say they remove offset b does that mean the line always goes through origin? like geometrically?
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: N/A — just a conceptual question, no code.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Confirmed yes, removing b forces the hyperplane to pass through origin. That made the whole paper click for me.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Re-read intro of Section 1 to confirm.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Trying to understand where the equality constraint in standard SVM dual comes from.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: why does standard SVM dual have that sum(alpha_i * y_i) = 0 constraint? does removing b actually make it disappear or do we still need it for something else
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Explained it comes from differentiating Lagrangian w.r.t. b. No b in primal = no constraint in dual. I then re-derived it myself on paper to confirm.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Checked Eq. (2) in the paper matches.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: for the coordinate update step im getting alpha_i_new = alpha_i + g_i / K_ii but im not sure where 1/K_ii comes from, is it just a learning rate or is it exact?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Confirmed it is the exact closed-form 1D maximizer, not a heuristic learning rate. Differentiating the 1D quadratic w.r.t. alpha_i gives step = g_i / K_ii before clipping.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Did the differentiation myself on paper.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Sanity check on my step order for the method description.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: my steps are: formulate dual → cold start → compute gradient → WSS → 1D update → update f values → KKT check → repeat. is this the right order or do I check KKT before WSS?
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Added the final prediction step which I had forgotten.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Pointed out I was missing the final output step (produce the sign(f(x)) classifier). Also confirmed KKT check is typically done before WSS to see if we can stop.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Cross-checked against Algorithm 1 in the paper.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Checking what KKT violation means for a single variable.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: whats the kkt violation for a single alpha_i? like how do i compute it as a number for each variable
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Wrote the KKT table in my own words in the notebook.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Gave the formula: violation = max(g_i, 0) if alpha_i < C, plus max(-g_i, 0) if alpha_i > 0.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Confirmed against Section 2.1 Eq. (6)-(8) of the paper.

top_5_prompts.prompt: why does standard SVM dual have that sum(alpha_i * y_i) = 0 constraint? does removing b actually make it disappear?
top_5_prompts.why_important: This is the core question of the whole paper. Understanding where that constraint comes from made everything else make sense.

top_5_prompts.prompt: im not sure where 1/K_ii comes from, is it just a learning rate or is it exact?
top_5_prompts.why_important: Needed to know this to correctly implement the update and explain it in the notebook.

top_5_prompts.prompt: when they remove b does the line always go through origin geometrically?
top_5_prompts.why_important: This basic geometric understanding set the foundation for Task 1.1 and also for designing the failure mode in Task 3.2.

top_5_prompts.prompt: whats the kkt violation for a single alpha_i, how do i compute it as a number
top_5_prompts.why_important: Needed this to implement and explain the stopping criterion correctly.

top_5_prompts.prompt: is this the right step order or do I check KKT before WSS?
top_5_prompts.why_important: Helped me get the algorithm flow right before writing it up.
